title: carthamus lanatus subsp. creticus
intro: Carthamus lanatus subsp. creticus, also known as Safflower, is a plant species belonging to the sunflower family and is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Safflower include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Safflower is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or oil, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
